2605.06619.title: Algospeak, Hiding in the Open: The Trade-off Between Legible Meaning and Detection Avoidance
2605.06619.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) increasingly mediate both content generation and moderation, linguistic evasion strategies known as Algospeak have intensified the coevolution between evaders and detectors. This research formalizes the underlying dynamics grounded in a joint action model: when Algospeak increases, detectability and understandability decrease. Further, the concept of Majority Understandable Modulation (MUM) is introduced and defined as the modulation level at which additional evasive alteration increases detector evasion but loses comprehension for the majority of recipients. To empirically probe this trade-off, we introduce a reproducible framework that can be used to create meaning-preserving, Algospeak-style variants, based on an existing taxonomy and with tunable modulation levels. Using COVID-19 disinformation as a first proof-by-example setting, we construct a reference dataset of 700 modulated items, drawn from twenty base sentences across five modulation levels and seven strategies. We then run two linked evaluations with seven different language models: one testing for interpretation through meaning recovery and one for disinformation detection through classification. Curve fitting over modulation levels yields an estimate of the Majority Understandable Modulation threshold and enables sensitivity analyses across strategies and models, see Figure 1. Results reveal the characteristic relationships between understandability and modulation. This study lays the groundwork for understanding the dynamics behind Algospeak and provides the framework, dataset, and experimental setups described.
2605.06619.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地介入內容生成和審核，稱為Algospeak的語言逃避策略加劇了逃避者與檢測者之間的共同演化。這項研究正式化了基於聯合行動模型的基本動態：當Algospeak增加時，檢測性和可理解性降低。此外，引入並定義了「多數可理解調變」（Majority Understandable Modulation, MUM）的概念，該概念指的是在這一調變水平上，額外的逃避性改變增加了檢測者的逃避，但對大多數接收者來說卻失去了理解。為了實證探討這一權衡，我們引入了一個可重複的框架，可以用來創建保持意義的Algospeak風格變體，基於現有的分類法並具有可調整的調變水平。以COVID-19虛假信息作為首個示例設置，我們構建了一個包含700個調變項目的參考數據集，這些項目來自五個調變水平和七種策略的二十個基本句子。我們隨後進行了兩次連結評估，使用七種不同的語言模型：一次測試通過意義恢復進行解釋，另一次則通過分類進行虛假信息檢測。對調變水平進行曲線擬合可估算多數可理解調變閾值，並使得對策略和模型的敏感性分析成為可能，見圖1。結果揭示了可理解性和調變之間的特徵關係。本研究為理解Algospeak背後的動態奠定了基礎，並提供了所描述的框架、數據集和實驗設置。
2605.06619.author: Jan Fillies et.al.
2605.06619.authors: Jan Fillies, Ronald E. Robertson, Jeffrey Hancock
